  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which primary focus do you want for “Check this”: (A) sentence-level wording/grammar and clarity, (B) logical consistency of the method and claims, or (C) novelty/positioning versus the cited prior work?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A “check” of this methods-style excerpt likely involves verifying that the training objective, supervision signal, and evaluation claims cohere logically.",
    "R1.6": "Brief requests like “Check this” commonly target proofreading and clarity at the sentence and paragraph level.",
    "RX.1": "The excerpt lacks full-paper context and results, so limits and assumptions should be explicitly acknowledged.",
    "RX.2": "Any assessment should avoid inflating claims about calibration or interpretability beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "The work should stay within the chosen checking focus and not drift into redesigning experiments or content outside the excerpt.",
    "RX.4": "Feedback should avoid encouraging reporting practices that could mislead about uncertainty or calibration quality.",
    "RX.5": "Observations should be tied to specific passages to maintain traceability to the provided artifact."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which primary focus do you want for “Check this”: (A) sentence-level wording/grammar and clarity, (B) logical consistency of the method and claims, or (C) novelty/positioning versus the cited prior work?

rationale.R1.2: A “check” of this methods-style excerpt likely involves verifying that the training objective, supervision signal, and evaluation claims cohere logically.
rationale.R1.6: Brief requests like “Check this” commonly target proofreading and clarity at the sentence and paragraph level.
rationale.RX.1: The excerpt lacks full-paper context and results, so limits and assumptions should be explicitly acknowledged.
rationale.RX.2: Any assessment should avoid inflating claims about calibration or interpretability beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: The work should stay within the chosen checking focus and not drift into redesigning experiments or content outside the excerpt.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid encouraging reporting practices that could mislead about uncertainty or calibration quality.
rationale.RX.5: Observations should be tied to specific passages to maintain traceability to the provided artifact.
